observed_facts: All 6 episodes triggered r_landing_success (terminal reward 200), but safe_contact_rate=0, stable_landing_rate=0
r_speed_penalty mean -0.0415, r_vertical_safety mean -0.0071 (trigger only near ground)
Hack score 0.2, shaping_goal_mismatch and unstable_contact_behavior flags true
likely_true_failures: Insufficient penalty or guidance for soft, stable ground contact
Mild speed penalty fails to dampen bouncing
Vertical safety activates too close to ground and only penalises vy
No component directly rewards or enforces stable ground contact without toggling
likely_false_positives: Shaping_goal_mismatch flag may be overly broad; terminal success is achieved and dense shaping exists
root_cause_hypotheses: Dense components do not adequately penalise high‑speed contact or contact interruptions; landing success event only requires 20‑step contact, allowing brief bounces before stabilising

lesson_assessments.assessment: strong_positive – successfully reduced center dominance and added descent guidance, providing a template for coupled rebalancing
weak_positive – weight increases and a mild global speed penalty improved scores only marginally; lesson warns that weight‑only tweaks are insufficient for bouncing problem

reusable_lessons: Decrease dominant component weight and introduce missing dense progress shaping
Coupled edits are more reliable than single changes
failed_or_weak_lessons: Merely increasing existing dense guidance weights or adding a mild global speed penalty does not resolve bouncing; a contact‑stability component is needed
avoid_actions: Merely increasing existing dense guidance weights or repeating the last weak edit without a new contact‑stability component
recommended_actions: Introduce a component that penalises contact toggling or rewards sustained stable contact
Strengthen speed penalties near ground (e.g., harsher near touchdown)
Consider adjusting vertical safety to include lateral velocity when near ground
Keep terminal success event, fuel penalty, and safety constraints preserved

reward_editor.rationale: To address unstable contact (bouncing) and insufficient soft‑landing shaping, introduce a dense contact‑stability bonus, replace global speed penalty with a near‑ground velocity penalty, and broaden vertical safety to include lateral velocity. Memory shows weight‑only edits are ineffective; this package adds new shaping while preserving all safety constraints.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Contact toggling should decrease significantly, stable landing rate should rise, speed near ground should lower, progress score should remain high. The new contact bonus (~0.05 weight) will encourage continuous ground contact, the near‑ground speed penalty will dampen high velocities at touchdown, and the expanded vertical safety will reduce lateral drift.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: If contact stability bonus is too strong, agent may never release contact and abort descent; careful scaling (weight 0.05) mitigates this. If speed penalty becomes too harsh near ground, descent may stall. Monitor contact toggles and crashes after training.

what_worked: Coupled rebalancing with a dedicated contact‑stability bonus and near‑ground velocity penalty
Broadening vertical safety to include lateral velocity
what_failed: Weight‑only increases on centering/descent without new shape do not fix bouncing
avoid_next: Merely increasing existing dense guidance weights or repeating the last weak edit without a contact‑stability component
recommend_next: Introduce a contact‑stability reward per step when both legs are on ground
Strengthen speed penalties specifically near the surface (y < 0.2)
Expand vertical safety to penalise lateral velocity
Monitor contact toggles and crash rates after training
applicability_notes: Applies when terminal landing reward is achieved but landing quality is poor (bouncing, high contact toggling)

diagnosis: Terminal landing achieved but unstable contact (bouncing) due to weak near‑ground speed control and no incentive for sustained contact. Package adds a per‑step contact‑stability bonus, a near‑ground velocity penalty, and lateral velocity awareness in vertical safety.

structural_analysis.missing_signal_hypothesis: Post‑landing stable ground contact is not explicitly shaped; the lander bounces after terminal contact because there is no penalty for residual velocity while firmly on the ground.
structural_analysis.why_local_edit_is_insufficient: Weight adjustments to existing speed or descent components cannot create a conditionally active penalty triggered only when both legs are touching and altitude is very low.
memory_constraints: Avoid component dominance – keep new component weight moderate (≤0.5).
Do not interfere with the descent phase – activate only when y<0.1 and both contacts hold.
safety_constraints: Use condition (both_contact AND y<0.1) to limit the penalty to certified ground contact.
Penalize only velocity, not engine thrust directly, to maintain fuel-efficiency incentives elsewhere.

reward_editor.rationale: A dense conditional penalty on velocity while firmly grounded discourages bouncing after landing, directly improving stable‑contact metrics without disrupting the existing descent progress or terminal success.

structural_search_response.diagnosis: Lander achieves terminal contact but lacks post‑landing stillness, causing repeated bounces and zero stable‑landing episodes. Adding a ground‑only velocity penalty closes this signal gap.
